The Danny Tanner (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 1 of 3
Speaker 1: All right, guys. Welcome to theme plate 18. This is the Danny Tanner, and for those of
you who aren't familiar, Danny Tanner was this fictional character played by Bob Saget
on the 90s show, Full House, who kind of became this caricature, this meme, memes
before memes were invented, but he became famous for his timely pep talks at the end
of an episode that usually resulted in acceptance, forgiveness, hugs, tears, and this
super cheesy redemption music. 20 years later, what does Danny Tanner have to do
with writing a sales email, and it's actually quite a lot. In the final hours of your launch,
your prospect is likely to be feeling a combination of fear, anxiety around the deadline,
self-judgment around not being in a position to pull the trigger soon, and a host of just
all this other stuff that's simply not conducive to the final conversion. Instead of
procrasti-shaming them and pouring the foam on even thicker, we actually exercise
some of our, get this, our human capacities, our human faculties of empathy to
acknowledge that this is all normal and congruent with what other people have bought
experienced, as well as a natural part of that overall process.
We also contrast the instantly curable pain or resistance around the buying decision
against the less curable pain in not deciding and resuming their old reality. It's a lot
easier to reverse the buying decision. If they come to the program, they're not getting
the results, it's just kind of not what they wanted, they can hit that undo button. If they
don't buy the program and they resume their old reality, the one that really wasn't
working for them, the one that wasn't serving, and our program is no longer available,
then that can't be reversed, so this creates an effect I call pain anchoring, the pain in
buying versus the pain of not buying, and when you could anchor one against the other,
it has a similar effect to price anchoring, making one seem a lot more valuable and
welcoming than the other, so that's what we're going to be doing in this one. Finally,
we'll build them back up by reminding our prospect of other wins and successes they've
had, and the positive qualities they've cultivated along the way. We're really going to
pump them up, show them that they do have these faculties and abilities lurking within
them, it's just time to channel it and express it in order to take that next step. That's it,
we're going to cue the cheesy music right now and step into the theme plate itself.
We're going to start off by reversing the procrasti-shame. Show them it's okay they
haven't enrolled yet while also drawing attention to the deadline, so this is where you
can get away with saying five hours left, because you're not saying, "Five hours left. You
suck. Why haven't you joined yet?" You're actually being a lot more empathetic and
grounded in the reality of the situation. You'll validate the resistance around investing.
Sure, it's scary. I get it. Drop that guarantee. Make it feel safer before you move forward
in this email and show that this resistance is still perfectly congruent with buying
behavior, maybe even a prerequisite for it, so don't exclude them because of this
resistance, see if you can find a way to include them. Lead into a call to action, and then
start comparing the resistance in buying to the pain around the moment of highest
tension that they'll continue to experience if they don't buy. This is where we are
anchoring the pain, making that fear and anxiety around the buying decision pale in
comparison what's going to happen if they have to just resume that old reality.
That will set us up for another call to action, and then we can go into the qualities you
admire or appreciate about them, so building them back up, showing them that they
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

The Danny Tanner (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 2 of 3
have these qualities inside of them, remind them of how those qualities have served
them in the past, and show them that those qualities will serve them now with the final
call to action. So, "What's up, Jen? Five hours left and I noticed you still haven't grabbed
your seat inside the Andy Yoga Hustle Master Class, so I just wanted to check in and say
that's totally cool." We're drawing attention to the deadline to create FOMO, but we're
not agitating it. People are agitated enough. They don't need us to agitate the anxiety
around FOMO any further. Instead, revalidate it. Revalidate that resistance instead of
shaming it. "Even with the 60 day W Yoga rate guarantee and all, it's still a biggish
decision," so dropping that guarantee. "In fact, if I remember correctly, some of our best
students waited until the final minutes before finally standing up and saying 'I'm ready,'"
so we're showing that this fear, this procrastination is perfectly congruent with being a
buyer.
"Not only that, but they came in with a lot of um stuff. Was this master class really
worth the investment? Can I really double my rate just by taking an online class? Will
those seven extra revenue streams I'll be learning about really work for me? What if I'm
not tech savvy. Is this going to stretch me too far outside my comfort zone? I don't want
to be pushy or have to sell. I just want to chill out, flow with life, and trust that it'll all
work out," so really kind of framing that internal dialogue, that this is all congruent with
being a buyer if not a prerequisite for being a buyer. "Sounding familiar yet? Here's the
thing: you're not meant to join the program with your stuff all figured out. This is a place
where you're invited to bring your stuff, your doubts, your fears, your sense that this
will never work for me, and simply surrender yourself to the proven road map that's
been working for hundreds of yoga teachers from literally every corner of the world. So
if you're ready to take the first step in ditching the yoga hustle, the running from studio
to studio, ignoring the fact that your Uber bill is more than your hourly rate," so pain
anchoring.
"That is part of that moment of highest tension, secretly sharing the same doubts that
your friends and family have expressed, although you'll never admit it to them out loud
and feeling generally unyogic about the fact that some teachers make it while others
don't. That's a secret, silent pain they have, part of that moment of highest tension that
I invite you to officially assume you'll crush by taking this special eight week journey
with me." So here's what we're doing. We've anchored the pain. Of course there is some
doubts and fears in joining, but they're allowed to bring that stuff. That's inclusive.
They're allowed to surrender it, and that's a lot easier than continuing on with the status
quo and continuing to experience that moment of highest tension. We've anchored that
pain. Here's that call to action to join the program, and then this is where you can
actually have your evergreen timers. Timers and scarcity and urgency aren't bad things.
They're bad when they exist and then you, as a human with empathy who can coach a
conversion besides to just reiterate what a timer says. In that case, you're not adding
any extra value. You're not actually coaching the conversion. You're not actually aware
of the context in your prospect.
You don't need to scare your prospect into a sale. If you've done your job up to now,
they're already fully aware of the consequences and the stakes, and they've generated
their own fear around it. Your job is to meet that and use that and alchemize that fear
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 3 --

The Danny Tanner (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 3 of 3
and anchor it against some other fears and coach them into that decisiveness. "Even
though the clock is ticking, here's why I'm not worried about you. You've been down this
path before. When the whole world told you that you should keep playing it safe instead
of venturing off to do this yoga thing, you still had the courage to pursue it. You know
how flipping rare and awesome that is? Even though you've had to contend with
probing questions and veiled smiles every time the career conversation comes up, you
still had the power and conviction to go with what, in your heart, you knew to be true.
Now, even though I'm inviting you to take a scary step forward in redefining yourself
and making the shift from underpaid, overworked yoga teacher to thriving, in demand
yoga professional, I trust that you'll consciously choose what feels right in your heart."
We're empowering them to make this decision. We're showing them that they've done
the scary thing before they've gone against the train. They've silenced the naysayers and
the doubters and they've done it anyway, so this is within them. They have the ability to
do it, it's just time for them to express it and consciously choose. "The only thing I ask is
that you actually choose," so once again binary choice. "Let's do this. I'm a bit scared,
but can't wait to give it a risk free shot," so inclusive. They're still a little scared, but they
could give it a risk free shot. It's all about that scale, that balance with fears and
resistance on one end and courage and excitement and safety on the other. We're not
looking for it to be fully balanced on one side. We're looking for it, for there to just be
enough courage and excitement and proof to weigh it down into the conversion versus,
"No, definitely not my vibe. Thanks but no thanks." "Either way, it's been such a
pleasure having you on this journey. If you make it into the master class, then I'll catch
up with you a little later. On the side of the Facebook group, drop me a PM to let me
know what you're most excited about. If not, looking forward to find new ways to serve
your awesome journey."
So that's it. We're also closing with that hyper specific and vivid future pace of the
ownership experience, making them feel safe, grounding it in what's actually going to
happen. They'll get their login details and stuff in the Facebook group and exchange a
few private messages on Facebook Messenger. That's a very familiar thing to this
audience. So, that is the Danny Tanner, a famous 90s dad coming back, not to haunt us
but to help us get higher conversions. So make good use of this one. Take about 20 to
25 minutes right now, sand timer style. Maybe pop into Spotify, watch a few Full House
clips if you feel like it on YouTube, and I will meet you back for the final theme plate in
just a few minutes. I'll see you in the next video.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
